body.title: Fix pre-existing _cortex AttributeError in test_spreading_activation.py
body.result: devstral fixed mock setup — 14 tests pass; MAX_TURNS hit before DONE: output
body.intention: I intend that test_empty_seeds_return_empty passes by fixing the Mock setup to include _cortex attribute.
body.description:
TestWordGraphSpreadFromWords::test_empty_seeds_return_empty fails with 'Mock object has no attribute _cortex' — the mock WordGraph instance doesn't have _cortex set. Pre-existing before T-spreading-activation-test-stale-path. The test at line 53 wraps spread_from_words via lambda but the underlying method accesses self._cortex on what is a Mock.

**Affected files:** tests/igor/test_spreading_activation.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN — fix mock setup so _cortex attribute exists. OUT — no production code changes.
**Completion criteria:** `python -m pytest tests/igor/test_spreading_activation.py -x -q` passes with 0 failures.

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** MAX_TURNS: hit 30 turns without DONE: prefix
**Where it broke:** max turns hit without completing
**What now?**
